I’ll inspect the current proof state against the prior failed review, then run the relevant Lean quality checks and map findings to each required criterion.

Quick update: I found the new theorem still appears to stop at an internal loop spec, so I’m now validating quality gates to confirm build/test status before final verdict.

## Success criteria from issue
Not met. `mod_pow_mont_correct` proves equality to `mont_pow_loop_spec` (`proofs/Gf2Core/Proofs/MontgomeryRoundtrip.lean:1178-1196`), but no theorem links that spec to `base^exp` in Montgomery form (`base^exp * R mod P`). The issue’s required terminal statement is missing.

## Separation of concerns
Pass. Changes are in `proofs/Gf2Core/Proofs/*` (Lean proof layer); no `gf2-core` ↔ `gf2-coding` dependency inversion seen.

## Functional paradigm and performance
Pass for scope (proof-only changes).

## Correctness
Partial. Loop/progress reasoning is solid (`Progress.lean:521-587`, guard handling at `MontgomeryRoundtrip.lean:1100-1163`), but end-to-end semantic correctness target is not established.

## Testing / linting
Could not run local checks (shell permission denied). Using provided gates/history: `lake-build` passed, `code-review` failed, `doc-review` pending. Therefore quality gates are not all green.

## Unsafe isolation
Pass. No unsafe additions; proof files only.

## Documentation
Insufficient for issue goal: theorem docs/state stop at internal spec, not the requested mathematical exponentiation result.

## Prior review feedback
Unresolved. Previous failure cited missing connection from loop spec to `base^exp` Montgomery semantics; this remains true.

## Issue dependencies
No dependencies listed; no upstream blocker.

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
